objective: Implement PU-015 by adding a Judge/PM audit packet and goal-closeout gate that can only support goal completion when runtime-card refs, delivery-truth verdicts, review-state, external-state, Linear state, validation receipts, root hygiene, reviewer artifacts, issue authority, and unresolved-risk classification are present, current, and separately proven.
deepModuleBoundary: src/lib/delivery-truth and src/lib/pr-closeout audit-readiness composition only; no public cockpit command and no external service mutation
implementationThesis: Represent Judge/PM readiness as an evidence-bound closeout packet, not a prose summary. The packet must fail closed when required proof surfaces are missing, stale, mismatched to the authorized issue/PR lifecycle, or still carry unresolved blockers. Existing delivery-truth and pr-closeout contracts should remain additive and advisory: they may block closeout, but they must not self-approve implementation completion or merge readiness.

currentEvidence.observed: delivery-truth already defines the goal_ready_for_judge_pm claim and supports receipt-backed status, freshness, head SHA, blocker class, and evidence use.
currentEvidence.gap: there is no first-class audit packet requiring all closeout surfaces before goal completion.

currentEvidence.observed: goal_ready_for_judge_pm currently requires pr_closeout evidence and applies receipt/freshness checks.
currentEvidence.gap: the evidence is still too coarse: it does not prove runtime card refs, delivery-truth verdicts, review-state, external-state, Linear state, root hygiene, reviewer artifacts, issue authority, and unresolved-risk classification as separate audit inputs.

currentEvidence.observed: pr-closeout blocks supplied delivery-truth verdicts for merge_ready, root_surface_tidy, remote_checks_current, review_threads_resolved, and linear_state_aligned.
currentEvidence.gap: goal_ready_for_judge_pm is not yet closeout-blocking, and there is no audit-readiness summary that ties required closeout verdicts to goal completion.

currentEvidence.observed: review-state validates reviewer artifact receipts for path, expected producer, head SHA, and non-zero size.
currentEvidence.gap: PU-015 still needs to require those artifacts as part of audit readiness rather than treating reviewer mailbox text or PR comments as sufficient.

currentEvidence.observed: goal status remains active and explicitly says Judge/PM-ready audit packet is required before completion.
currentEvidence.gap: there is no repo code or fixture that prevents an implementation from claiming the goal complete without the packet.

inScope: src/lib/review-state/** only if existing reviewer-artifact validation must be reused without expanding packet shape
src/lib/external-state/** only if existing snapshot freshness helpers must be reused without expanding packet shape
focused fixtures and tests for Judge/PM audit readiness
outOfScope: creating a public harness audit command unless existing code proves no private verifier seam can express the gate
performing external writes to GitHub, Linear, CodeRabbit, CircleCI, Socket, Snyk, or Semgrep
claiming PR #305 merge readiness while reviewDecision is CHANGES_REQUESTED, mergeStateStatus is BLOCKED, Socket is pending, or any current-head required check is unresolved
treating reviewer mailbox text, chat summaries, or PR comments as reviewer artifact proof when an artifact path was requested
embedding raw transcripts, prompts, secrets, credentials, bulky telemetry, or full review-comment bodies in audit packets
changing harness next from advisory cockpit into executable authority
absorbing unrelated dirty Project Brain, agent-readiness, media, research, or .harness knowledge changes into PU-015 unless they become explicitly reviewed intent scope
acceptanceCriteria: A typed Judge/PM audit packet or equivalent private verifier input requires runtimeCardRefs, deliveryTruthVerdicts, reviewStateRef, externalStateRef, linearStateRef or explicit not-applicable authority, validationReceiptRefs, rootHygieneRef, reviewerArtifactRefs, requiredReviewerRoles, issueAuthorityMap, unresolvedRiskClassifications, generatedAt, verifiedAt, headSha, and schemaVersion.

Audit readiness derives reviewer artifact requirements from a canonical requiredReviewerRoles list supplied by lifecycle policy or verifier configuration; packet builders cannot shrink the required set by omitting a role from reviewerArtifactRefs.

Audit readiness fails when any mandated reviewer role is absent, any required reviewer artifact path is missing, zero bytes, mismatched to expected producer/role, not bound to the current head SHA, or only present as mailbox/status text.

issueAuthorityMap has an explicit minimum shape: lifecycleIssueId, parentIssueId or parentNotApplicable decision, prNumber or prNotApplicable decision, externalGoalId or externalGoalNotApplicable decision, authorityOwner, decisionSourceRef, decidedAt, and rationale. Any not-applicable entry requires owner, rationale, and timestamp.
Audit readiness fails when any required delivery-truth verdict is missing, non-current, non-passing, not claim-supporting, unsupported for the claim source, or attached to a different head SHA.

Audit readiness fails when external-state or review-state evidence is stale, missing verifier-owned fetch proof, orientation-only, mismatched to the active repository/PR/head, or not linked by receipt reference.
Audit readiness fails when Linear or tracker state is absent without an explicit issueAuthorityMap not-applicable decision and owner.
Audit readiness fails when the claimed closeout issue, parent issue, PR number, or external goal does not match issueAuthorityMap.
Audit readiness fails when unresolved risks are unclassified, use unknown blocker classes, or omit owner and next action.
goal_ready_for_judge_pm becomes closeout-blocking in pr-closeout delivery-truth summary unless the verifier proves it is pass/current/claim-supporting.

A deterministic goal-status transition check blocks any docs/goals/codex-runtime-evidence-verifier-cockpit/state.yaml transition to complete unless the goal board references a pass/current/claim-supporting Judge/PM audit packet or records an explicitly authorized blocked status.

The gate is agent-discoverable: implementation must expose a documented invocation path or private verifier function used by pr-closeout/delivery-truth flow, with machine-readable blocking output that agents can cite without reading prose.

Tests cover missing reviewer artifact, stale audit packet, unresolved blocker, mismatched issue ID, missing Linear/PR linkage, stale head SHA, and a passing happy path with separate evidence surfaces.
No implementation path can mark this lifecycle goal complete from local validation, CI green state, merged PR state, or a prose audit summary alone.

automationPlan.name: intent artifact JSON validation
proves: PU-015 intent artifact is syntactically valid JSON before review

automationPlan.name: pre-implementation intent review gate
proves: PU-015 implementation cannot start until reviewed intent artifacts exist and reviewStatus has been deliberately advanced to pass

automationPlan.name: focused PU-015 verifier tests
automationPlan.command: pnpm vitest run src/lib/delivery-truth/*.test.ts src/lib/pr-closeout*.test.ts src/lib/pr-closeout/*.test.ts
proves: delivery-truth and pr-closeout audit-readiness behavior for PU-015

automationPlan.name: reviewer artifact existence checks
automationPlan.command: repo-owned verifier fixture or script iterates the requiredReviewerRoles list and fails when any mandated artifact ref is missing, zero bytes, producer/role mismatched, or head-SHA mismatched
proves: all lifecycle-mandated audit reviewer artifacts exist before any PU-015 done or audit-ready claim

automationPlan.name: goal-status transition guard
automationPlan.command: repo-owned goal-board or delivery-truth fixture attempts a complete transition without Judge/PM audit evidence and expects failure
proves: goal completion cannot bypass the Judge/PM audit packet by mutating goal state directly

automationPlan.name: goal board validation
proves: goal state and receipts remain valid after PU-015 updates

reviewPlan.artifactNamingNote: PU-specific review artifact names intentionally override default reviewer filenames so the goal board can tie findings to this lifecycle unit.

postReviewMutationPolicy: any material mutation to objective, implementationThesis, currentEvidence, inScope, outOfScope, guardedPathGlobs, acceptanceCriteria, automationPlan, reviewPlan, stopConditions, or rollback resets reviewStatus to pending

parallelPrTriageStrategy.default: allow read-only PR triage to monitor PR #305 while the coordinator performs PU-015 intent review and implementation only when touched files do not overlap a current-head PR blocker
parallelPrTriageStrategy.parallelCodeWorkAllowed: allowed only for private audit-readiness verifier work after intent review passes and when PR #305 blockers remain review/process/external-state only
blockedWhen: PR #305 reports a current-head failure in delivery-truth, pr-closeout, review-state, external-state, runtime-card, goal-board, or docs-gate surfaces touched by PU-015
intent reviewers identify a material closeout authority, issue authority, stale-state, or artifact-proof risk not patched in intent
implementation would require external writes, self-approval, public command exposure, or merging unrelated dirty worktree lanes
parallelPrTriageStrategy.safeFallback: continue read-only PR triage, fixture design, and intent refinement without touching production audit-readiness code

stopConditions: Stop if adversarial or agent-native intent review reports a material blocker and the intent has not been patched and re-reviewed.
Stop if the audit packet can pass with reviewer mailbox text but no non-empty reviewer artifact.
Stop if goal_ready_for_judge_pm can pass without separate current delivery-truth, review-state, external-state, Linear/tracker, validation, root-hygiene, and issue-authority evidence.
Stop if stale, orientation-only, missing, unknown, or head-mismatched evidence can support audit readiness.
Stop if local tests, CI green state, resolved review threads, or PR mergeability alone can mark the lifecycle complete.
Stop if issueAuthorityMap mismatches the claimed issue, parent issue, PR number, or external goal and the verifier does not block closeout.
Stop if unresolved risk classes can be omitted, unknown, ownerless, or next-actionless.
Stop if implementation touches unrelated Project Brain, agent-readiness, media, research, or .harness knowledge files without a reviewed PU-015 scope update.
Stop if implementation needs to mutate Codex source or external services outside the Harness-only contract.
rollback: Remove audit-readiness packet integration only if an equivalent stricter goal-closeout gate replaces it.
Revert goal_ready_for_judge_pm closeout-blocking behavior only if it creates duplicate blockers and tests preserve an independent Judge/PM gate.
Keep negative fixtures for missing reviewer artifacts, stale audit packets, unresolved blockers, mismatched issue IDs, and missing Linear/PR linkage unless superseded by stronger tests.
Do not modify or roll back /Users/jamiecraik/dev/codex because PU-015 is Harness-only.
assumptions: JSC-363 remains the lifecycle owner for this implementation goal.
PU-015 should prefer private verifier composition and fixtures over adding public CLI surface.
Existing delivery-truth, pr-closeout, review-state, and external-state modules are the correct deep-module placement for PU-015.
A stale or pending PR #305 external check may orient the coordinator but cannot support goal completion.
The current dirty Project Brain and agent-readiness worktree changes are outside PU-015 until separately scoped and reviewed.
